content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 2.1 WARN — description claims "Built-in ad blocker" and "AI-powered suggestions" (fastlane/metadata/en-US/description.txt:1-3), but the only source file (App/ContentView.swift) is a simple in-memory task list with no content-blocker extension, ML/CoreML imports, or suggestion logic.
Pierre: Apple rejects apps under 2.1 when marketing copy promises features reviewers cannot find in the binary. Here the metadata promises an ad blocker and AI predictions, yet the code contains only a TextField and a List — no NEContentBlocker/SFContentBlocker target, no CoreML/CreateML, no prediction code. Either implement these features before submission or strip the claims from the description; the "works fully offline" claim is trivially true but the other two will likely trigger rejection.
